title: ConvertKit vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and GetResponse. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your needs best.
h1: ConvertKit vs GetResponse: Complete Comparison for 2025
intro: ConvertKit and GetResponse are both freemium email marketing platforms designed for content creators, but they take different approaches to solving marketing challenges. ConvertKit focuses on simplicity for creators and bloggers, while GetResponse offers a broader suite of tools including webinars and online courses. This guide will help you determine which platform aligns best with your business goals.
choose_a_if: You prioritize a clean, creator-focused interface designed specifically for bloggers and writers
You need strong ecommerce capabilities and commerce features
You want the highest affiliate commission at 30% recurring for 24 months
choose_b_if: You want a lower starting price and need to host webinars or online courses
You're building sales funnels and need conversion-focused automation tools
You prefer a platform that's been established longer (since 1998) with broader feature integration

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit excels for creators focused on email and simple automation with an intuitive interface. GetResponse offers more comprehensive features including webinars and courses at a lower price. For most creators, GetResponse provides better overall value, but ConvertKit is superior if simplicity and email focus matter most.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is cheaper, starting at $19/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Additionally, GetResponse's free tier supports up to 500 subscribers while ConvertKit offers 1,000, making ConvertKit more generous for bootstrapped creators. However, GetResponse includes more advanced features in lower-tier plans.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and email content. Most email marketing platforms provide standard export formats (CSV, etc.) that the other can import. However, you'll need to manually recreate automations and sequences, as these don't transfer directly between platforms.
